_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites 'text' from live numbers; these are the computed fallbacks so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.cape: The Shiller CAPE ratio stands at 40.7, more than double its 140-year average of 17.3, and close to the all-time high of 44.19 seen in December 1999, signaling a market priced for extreme optimism. Such stretched valuations have historically preceded major corrections.
notes.buffett: The Buffett Indicator, which compares total market value to GDP, is an unprecedented 231.7%, far exceeding the dot-com bubble peak of 146% and the 2007 peak of 110%, highlighting a market vastly overvalued relative to the economy.
notes.concentration: Market concentration is intense, with the top 5 companies making up nearly 30% of the S&P 500 and the tech sector commanding 48% of the index, while the top 10 firms hold 41.0%, up from 17.5% in 2015, increasing systemic risk from a few dominant players.
notes.yieldcurve: The yield curve spread between 10-year and 3-month Treasuries is a modest 0.55 points, while the New York Fed assigns a 31% chance of recession, reflecting underlying economic concerns despite still positive long-term rates.
notes.drypowder: Record cash reserves of $397.4 billion at Berkshire Hathaway reflect significant 'dry powder' or ready capital, yet 79% of institutions expect asset prices to decline, indicating widespread caution even amid high valuations.
notes.finale: Between December 2026 and May 2027, IPO lock-up expirations for giants like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic could flood the market with shares, draining liquidity and potentially triggering sharp price adjustments in an already stretched market.
